description: A handler authenticates the user but never checks ownership of the client-supplied resource id, allowing one user to read or modify another's data (IDOR).
body: Failure mode: an endpoint trusts a valid session as sufficient and fetches/updates a record by an id taken straight from the request, so any authenticated user can substitute another id and access foreign data. Detection signals: a test where user A passes user B's id succeeds; no ownership/tenant predicate near the query; access logs show cross-account ids. Do not treat route-level auth as object-level authorization. Safer fallback: load the object, verify the principal's permission against it server-side, deny by default, and add a negative cross-user test.
